The theme includes full built-in support for Shopify gift cards. Customers can buy a gift card for themselves to use at checkout, or send one directly to someone else with a personal message and a scheduled delivery date. Once purchased, the recipient gets their own gift card page showing the code, the balance, and a QR code they can scan at checkout.
Setting Up a Gift Card Product

Before anything shows up on your store, you need to create a gift card product in your Shopify Admin. The theme reads this product automatically  -  there are no extra theme settings to configure for the gift card product page itself.

items: Go to Shopify Admin → Products.
Click Add product.
Under the product type, select Gift card - this is a special product type in Shopify.
Set the title, description, and images as you would any other product.
Add variants for each denomination you want to offer  -  for example $10, $50, $100, $300.
Save the product and make sure it is set to Active.

contents.content: Shopify handles all the gift card balance tracking, delivery, and redemption. The theme simply displays the product and the gift card details page in a way that matches your store's design.
The Gift Card Product Page

When a customer visits the gift card product page, it looks and works like any other product page in the theme. The main difference is that it includes an optional recipient form for sending the card as a gift. The gift card variants appear as selectable options  -  for example $10.00, $50.00, $100.00, and $300.00.

Buying for Yourself: If the customer just wants a gift card for their own use, they skip the recipient form entirely. They choose a denomination, set the quantity, and add to cart or buy now. After checkout, Shopify sends the gift card code to their own email.

contents.label: Sending as a Gift
items: To send the gift card to someone else, the customer ticks the "I want to send this as a gift" checkbox. This expands a form with the following fields.
Recipient Email: Required. The email address the gift card will be delivered to. The form will not allow checkout without a valid email address entered here.
Recipient Name: Optional. The name of the person receiving the gift card.
Message: Optional. A personal message included with the gift card delivery. Limited to 200 characters.
Send On: Optional. A date picker that lets the customer schedule when the gift card is delivered. If left blank, it is sent immediately after purchase.

contents.content: The Gift Card Redemption Page
After a gift card is purchased and delivered, the recipient receives an email from Shopify with a link to their personal gift card page. This page is styled to match your store.

contents.label: What the Recipient Sees
items: Store logo: Your store's logo or name appears in the top left of the card header.
QR code: A scannable QR code is displayed at the top of the card. Customers can scan this directly at checkout on compatible devices.
Gift card name: The name of the gift card product is shown below the QR code.
Current balance: The remaining balance on the card is shown prominently and updates every time the card is used.
Expiry notice: If the gift card has an expiry date, a badge shows when it expires. If it has already expired or been disabled, a badge shows that too.
Gift card code: The unique gift card code is displayed inside a dashed box. Customers can select and copy it manually.
Copy Code button: A one-click button that copies the code to the clipboard. The button briefly changes to "Copied!" to confirm the action.
Visit store link: A link at the bottom takes the recipient directly to your store to start shopping.

contents.content: Customers apply a gift card code at Shopify's checkout page by entering it in the gift card or discount code field. If the order total is higher than the gift card balance, the customer pays the difference with another payment method. If the balance is higher than the order total, the remaining amount stays on the card for future use.
Best Practices

contents.label: Do
items: Add a clear, attractive image to your gift card product  -  it appears on the product page and is the first thing customers see.
Offer a range of denominations so customers can choose an amount that suits them. Common options are a low, mid, and high value.
Test the gifting flow yourself before publishing  -  buy a gift card, send it to your own email, and confirm the recipient page looks correct.
Keep your gift card product title short and descriptive, such as "Store Gift Card" or "Premium Gift Card", since the title appears on the recipient's gift card page.

contents.label: Don't
items: Set a gift card product as a Draft  -  customers will not be able to find or purchase it.
Add the gift card to a collection that also contains regular products if you want to keep the shopping experience clean. Consider a separate "Gift Cards" collection.
Worry about the recipient form fields being submitted empty  -  Name, Message, and Send On are all optional. Only the Recipient Email is required when the "send as a gift" checkbox is ticked.

contents.content: 404 Page
A fully designed two-column 404 layout with a brand image on the left and an editable message, button, and status code on the right.

The 404 page appears when a customer tries to visit a URL that does not exist in your store  -  for example a deleted product, a mistyped link, or an old URL that no longer works. Instead of showing a plain error message, this theme gives you a fully designed two-column layout with an image on the left and your message and button on the right. Everything on this page is editable from the Theme Editor.
Section Settings

contents.label: General
items: Color Scheme: Sets the background and text colors for the page. Pick a scheme that matches the rest of your store.
Padding Top / Padding Bottom: Controls the space inside the section, above and below the content.
Margin Top / Margin Bottom: Controls the space outside the section, pushing it away from whatever is above or below it on the page.

contents.label: Image
items: Image: The image that fills the left half of the page. Use something visually interesting that fits your brand - a product photo, a lifestyle shot, or a branded illustration all work well here. If left empty, the theme shows a placeholder graphic automatically. Upload a high-resolution image for the best result - a portrait or square image works better than a very wide one.

contents.label: Content
items: Top Heading: The short line of text that appears at the top right of the page, above the button. Typically a friendly message explaining the page could not be found. Keep it brief so it reads clearly at a glance.
Button Label: The text shown on the call-to-action button next to the top heading. Default: "Back to homepage".
Button Link: Where the button takes the customer. Default: your homepage. You can change it to any page in your store, such as your all-products collection.
404 Heading: The large text displayed at the bottom left of the content area. Default: "Page Not Found".
Show Status Code: Toggles the large error number on or off. When turned on, the number sits in the bottom right corner of the content area as a bold visual element.
404 Code: The number displayed when Show Status Code is turned on. Default: 404. Most stores keep this as 404 since that is the standard error code customers recognise.

contents.content: Best Practices

contents.label: Do
items: Use a striking product or brand image on the left side  -  the 404 page is a chance to keep customers engaged even when something has gone wrong.
Point the button to a useful destination  -  your all-products collection or a popular category page is often more helpful than the homepage.
Keep the Top Heading message short, friendly, and honest. Customers appreciate clarity over clever phrasing when they are lost.

contents.label: Don't
items: Leave the button link empty  -  it defaults to the homepage, which is fine, but if you have a better destination for lost customers, set it explicitly.
Use a very wide landscape image  -  the image column is tall and portrait-oriented, so wide images will be cropped heavily on the top and bottom.
Turn off the status code if your store targets customers who are likely to share or troubleshoot broken links  -  the 404 number helps them identify the issue quickly.

contents.content: 404 Collections List
Show up to four collection cards on your 404 page so customers always have a helpful next step when they land on a broken or missing link.

The 404 Collections List section is designed specifically for your store's 404 page. Instead of leaving customers on a dead end, this section shows up to four of your collections with their product count and a button to browse all collections. Each collection card shows a product image, the collection name, and the number of products in that collection. When a customer hovers over a card, the image animates with a subtle tilt and lift effect. The entire card is clickable and takes the customer directly to that collection.
How to Edit the 404 Collections List Section
Section Settings

items: Color Scheme: Choose a color scheme that matches the rest of your store.
Section Heading: The title shown above the collection cards - for example "Some of our collections" or "Keep exploring".
Button Label: The text shown on the button below the collection cards. Default: "View collections".
Button Link: Where the button takes the customer when clicked. Default: your all-products page. You can change this to any page, collection, or custom URL.
Layout & Spacing: Controls the padding and margin around the section. Only set the desktop value - the section scales it down automatically for smaller screens.

contents.content: Collection Blocks
Each Showcase Collection block is one card in the grid. You can add up to 4 blocks. Click Add block to add a new one.

items: Select Collection: Pick which Shopify collection this card represents. The card will automatically show the collection's featured image, the collection name, and the number of products in the collection (shown as a small superscript count next to the title, e.g. (06), (11)).

contents.content: Collection images come from the collection itself in Shopify. Go to Shopify Admin → Products → Collections, open a collection, and upload a featured image there. The card will automatically use that image. If no image is set, a placeholder illustration is shown. The "Add block" option is greyed out once 4 blocks have been added.
Fallback Behaviour

If no blocks have been added yet, the section automatically looks for collections in your store named "Vans", "Jordan", "Nike air", and "Converse". If it finds them, it shows them as cards. If not, it shows placeholder cards with mock titles and counts so the section always looks complete in the theme editor and during setup. Once you add your own blocks, the fallback disappears and only your chosen collections are shown.
Best Practices

contents.label: Do
items: Add a featured image to each collection in Shopify Admin so the cards look polished  -  placeholder illustrations look unfinished on a live store.
Use collections that are popular or relevant to what most customers are looking for.
Keep the section heading short and reassuring  -  customers are on a broken page, so a message like "Here's what you might be looking for" works better than a generic title.
Set the button link to your most relevant browse destination, whether that is all products, a specific category, or a sale collection.

contents.label: Don't
items: Add collections with very few products  -  a count of (01) or (02) can feel sparse and underwhelming.
Leave the button label as the default if it does not match your store's tone.
Use more than 4 blocks  -  the grid layout is built for exactly 4 cards and adding more is not supported.
Leave this section off your 404 page entirely  -  a helpful 404 page significantly reduces the chance customers leave your store after hitting a broken link.

contents.content: Password Page
A fully branded page for visitors when your store is password protected  -  with an email waitlist signup, a password entry modal, and editable headings and button labels.

The Password Page is what visitors see when your store is password protected  -  typically when your store is still under development or has not yet launched. Instead of showing a blank screen or a generic Shopify lock page, this theme gives you a fully branded page where visitors can sign up to be notified when you open, and where you or your team can enter a password to access the store.
How to Enable Password Protection

The password itself is not set inside the Theme Editor. To turn password protection on and set the password, go to Shopify Admin → Online Store → Preferences and scroll to the Password protection section. Enable it there and set your password. Once that is done, the theme's Password Page will show automatically for all visitors.
Section Settings

contents.label: Main Page Content
items: Color Scheme: Sets the background and text colors for the password page. Pick a scheme that matches your brand.

Heading: The large text displayed at the center of the page. Use something that sets the right tone - for example "Opening soon!" or "We're getting ready". If you have set a password message in Shopify Admin under Online Store → Preferences, that message will show here instead.
Subheading: A shorter line of supporting text shown below the heading - for example "Be the first one to know when we open!" or "Sign up below and we will let you know the moment we go live."
Subscribe Button Label: The text on the button next to the email input field. Default: "Subscribe".

contents.content: Email Subscription

The center of the page shows an email input field and a subscribe button. Visitors can enter their email address to be notified when the store opens. When they submit their email, Shopify saves it as a newsletter subscriber and shows a confirmation message in place of the form. You can view these subscribers in your Shopify Admin under Customers.
Header Button and Password Modal

items: Header Button Label: The text of the button in the top right corner of the page that opens the password entry panel. Default: "Enter storefront password".
Password Modal Button Label: The text of the button inside the password panel next to the input field. Default: "Enter".

contents.content: When the correct password is entered, the store opens normally. If the wrong password is entered, an error message appears below the input field and the panel stays open for another attempt.
Best Practices

contents.label: Do
items: Set a clear, friendly heading that tells visitors the store is coming soon  -  leaving them guessing creates a poor first impression.
Use the subheading to encourage email sign-ups. The email list you build on the password page is valuable for your launch announcement.
Keep your storefront password private  -  only share it with people who genuinely need access, such as team members or a client reviewing the build.
Test the email subscription form before your launch to make sure sign-ups are coming through to your Customers list in Shopify Admin.

contents.label: Don't
items: Forget to disable password protection before launching  -  go to Shopify Admin → Online Store → Preferences and turn it off when you are ready to go live.
Leave the heading as the default if your store has a specific name or theme  -  a personalised message feels much more intentional.
Use the storefront password as your Shopify Admin password  -  they are separate and should be kept that way.

contents.content: Privacy Policy
Effective Date: May 01, 2026
1. Introduction

Rico ("we," "our," or "the App") provides currency localization services for Shopify merchants store. We are committed to a privacy policy. Our app is built to function with minimal data interaction, ensuring a fast and secure experience for both merchants and their global customers.
2. Merchant Information (Data We Collect)
To provide our services, we collect limited administrative data from merchants:

items: Shopify Store ID and Domain: To identify your account and apply settings.
Contact Information: Email addresses provided via Shopify for support
Theme Access: We use "Theme Embed" permissions to render the currency switcher script on your storefront.

contents.content: 3. Customer Data & Geolocation

items: We do not collect, store, or share Personal Identifiable Information (PII) from your customers.

contents.content: Real-Time Geolocation: To display local pricing, the App processes the visitor's IP address in real-time. This processing happens "in-memory" to determine the country code and is not stored on our servers.
Browser Local Storage: We utilize the customer's browser local storage only to remember their preferred currency. This ensures a consistent experience as they navigate your store.

Smart Updates: While the App automatically detects location, customers always have the option to switch currencies manually. The local storage is automatically updated to reflect the most recent exchange rates based on merchant settings.
4. Global Regulatory Compliance
We adhere to the highest international data protection standards to ensure your store remains compliant and secure worldwide. Our operations are aligned with the following regulations:

GDPR (European Union): We operate as a Data Processor under the General Data Protection Regulation. We process data based on the "Legitimate Interest" of providing a functional, localized storefront. We do not transfer or sell European user data to any third parties. Official Reference: https://gdpr-info.eu/

CCPA/CPRA (United States): We do not "sell" or "share" customer personal information as defined by California law. Our app respects user privacy settings and allows customers to opt-out of tracking through your store's primary privacy banner. Official Reference: https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa
5. Data Security & Storage

Because we do not store customer profiles or order histories, the risk of data breach is significantly minimized. Merchant account data is stored on secure, encrypted servers and is only accessible by authorized personnel for support purposes.
6. Your Rights (Merchant & Customer)

Merchants: You may request to view, update, or delete your account data at any time by contacting us. And when you uninstall this app, we keep your data analytics for some time, but settings and other things are not kept.

Customers: Since we don't save any customer information, we don't have any records to show or delete. This means your customer data stays safe and compliant because there is no customer data for us to share or lose.
7. Data Security
At Rico, we prioritize the security of your store. Our infrastructure is built on the principle of Data Minimization.
Merchant Protection: We only store essential merchant data required to keep the service running. This information is encrypted and stored on secure servers with restricted access.

No Customer Data Collection: We do not collect, store, or log any personal information from your customers. While our app detects a visitor's location to switch currencies, this process happens instantly in real-time and is never saved to a database.
Safe & Non-Intrusive: Because we never touch customer PII (Personally Identifiable Information), your store remains fully compliant with global privacy standards without any extra risk.
8. Changes to this Policy
We may update this policy to reflect new global regulations. We encourage merchants to review this page periodically. Continued use of the App after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
9. Children's Privacy
Our service is designed exclusively for Shopify merchants and their commercial operations. Rico does not knowingly collect, request, or store any data from children.

Because our app functions strictly as a currency switching tool and does not require user accounts or personal profiles from store visitors, we do not process any "Personally Identifiable Information" (PII) from children or any other age group. We are fully committed to complying with the Children's Online Privacy.
10. Contact Us
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your store's data, please contact our dedicated support team:

contents.content: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Here are some frequently asked questions about the Rico theme and how it works.

contents.heading: How do I install the Rico theme on my Shopify store?
contents.desc: Go to Online Store → Themes in your Shopify admin, click Add theme → Visit Theme Store, find Rico, and click Try theme. Once you're satisfied, click Buy and then Publish to make it live.

contents.heading: Can I preview the theme before purchasing?
contents.desc: Yes. You can start a free trial directly from the Shopify Theme Store. Any customizations you make during the trial carry over if you decide to purchase.

contents.heading: How do I customize the colors and fonts?
contents.desc: Open the Theme Editor (Online Store → Themes → Customize) and go to Theme Settings → Colors or Typography. You can create multiple color schemes and assign them to individual sections across your store.

contents.heading: What video types does the theme support?
contents.desc: Rico supports three video types: Shopify-hosted videos, external YouTube or Vimeo URLs (including YouTube Shorts), and direct MP4 URLs. You can set separate desktop and mobile videos for sections like Hero Banner and Promotion Banner.

contents.heading: How does the Mega Menu work?
contents.desc: Add a Mega Menu block inside the Header section and enter the exact navigation item name in the Nav Link Title field. The Mega Menu supports category links, brand links, featured products, a promotional banner image, and a scrolling marquee.

contents.heading: How do I set up the Bundle Builder discount tiers?
contents.desc: You need to complete three steps: create a collection of bundle products in Shopify, create matching discount codes in Shopify Admin under Discounts, then add Discount Tier blocks in the Bundle Builder section with the same minimum item counts, percentages, and codes. All three steps must match for discounts to apply at checkout.

contents.heading: How do I add upsell products to a product page?
contents.desc: In Shopify Admin, open the product, locate the Related Products metafield, and select the products you want to recommend. Then add the Upsell Block to the Product section in the Theme Editor.

contents.heading: How does the countdown timer on the product page work?
contents.desc: The countdown block requires three product metafields to be filled in: Discount ending time (custom.discount_ending_time), Discount code (custom.discount_code), and Discount title (custom.discount_tittel). If any of these are empty, the block will not appear.

contents.heading: Can I show different products on mobile and desktop?
contents.desc: The theme supports separate desktop and mobile media for sections like Hero Banner and Promotion Banner. You can upload different images or videos for each device size. If no mobile media is provided, the desktop version is used as a fallback.

contents.heading: What browsers does Rico support?
contents.desc: Rico targets current versions of Safari, Chrome, Firefox, and Edge on desktop, and Mobile Safari, Chrome Mobile, and Samsung Internet on mobile. Test major changes in your target browsers before publishing.

contents.heading: How do I contact support?
contents.desc: In the Theme Editor, open Theme actions in the top bar and choose Get support. Support covers questions about Rico settings and sections, troubleshooting theme behavior, and guidance on using the theme editor. Custom code changes and third-party app integrations are not covered.
